steps.title: Read circle A radius
steps.content:
A circle equation has the form
$$(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$$For circle A, the right side is $4$, so
$$r_A^2=4$$and $r_A=2$.

steps.title: Square the doubled radius
steps.content:
Circle B has radius twice as large:
$$r_B=2\cdot 2=4$$In the equation for circle B, $k$ is the square of the radius:
$$k=4^2=16$$The answer is 16.
